candidate_llm_application: AKHA-GATE-FILTER — dual-head FROZEN input-gate binary classifier. Incoming prompt p encoded by small frozen encoder E. Two independent heads: H_benign(E(p)) admission-friendliness, H_malign(E(p)) injection-suspicion. Admission rule: pass to base LLM iff H_benign>tau_b AND H_malign<tau_m; else route to safety-handler. Gate weights frozen during base-model FT.
rule: hit iff kw_count>=2 OR sem_score>=0.7 OR func_score>=0.7; total_hits>=1 => FAIL

results.title: Llama Guard Meta — instruction-tuned input+output safety classifier; outputs safe/unsafe + violated category
results.rationale: EXACT-TWIN. Llama Guard is the canonical frozen input-gate safety classifier deployed as a separate model that classifies incoming prompts as safe/unsafe before passing to base LLM. The admission rule (pass iff classifier says safe, else route to safety handler) is identical to AKHA-GATE-FILTER's pass-or-route logic. Llama Guard already operates as a frozen gate independent of base-model fine-tuning. AKHA's dual-head (benign + malign) is a trivial decomposition of Llama Guard's multi-category output into two binary heads. Akha hill-tribe spirit-gate cultural framing supplies narrative, not mechanism. Keyword overlap on 'frozen' / 'input-gate' / 'binary classifier' concepts.

results.title: Llama Guard 4 12B Multimodal — prompt + response safety with violated-category
results.rationale: Llama Guard 4 directly subsumes AKHA-GATE-FILTER: frozen multimodal input safety classifier with category-level output that gates prompt admission to base LLM. The category-level output trivially decomposes into the benign/malign dual-head form AKHA proposes (one head over benign-allowed categories, one over malign-violation categories). Frozen-during-base-FT is the standard deployment pattern. AKHA's threshold-based admission rule (H_benign>tau_b AND H_malign<tau_m) is just Llama Guard's binary safe/unsafe verdict expressed as a conjunction of two thresholded scores.

results.title: NeMo Guardrails 2026 — production runtime safety rails for self-hosted LLMs
results.rationale: NeMo Guardrails is the production runtime framework that implements exactly AKHA's pattern: frozen input filters with admission/block decisions and routing of blocked prompts to safety handlers. NeMo's input rails operate as binary classifiers that gate base-LLM access without affecting base-model FT, matching AKHA's frozen-during-FT requirement. The dual-head benign+malign decomposition is already an idiomatic NeMo input-rail pattern (allow-list rail + jailbreak-detection rail combined via AND). Propagate-or-block boundary admission is the canonical NeMo input rail semantics.

results.title: PromptGuard 4-layer (Nature SciReports 2025) — input gatekeeping + MiniBERT detection; 67% reduction
results.rationale: EXACT-TWIN. PromptGuard's MiniBERT (small frozen encoder) + input gatekeeping is identical to AKHA's small frozen encoder E + admission rule. The 4-layer architecture decomposes into multiple independent classification heads operating on the same encoder representation, matching AKHA's dual-head (H_benign, H_malign) over shared E(p). Frozen MiniBERT during downstream FT is the same frozen-input-gate property. Keyword overlap matches >=2: 'input-gate' + 'binary classifier' + 'frozen' + 'propagate-or-block boundary admission' concepts all map directly. AKHA's only delta is using two heads instead of four — strict simplification, not novelty.

results.title: Bypassing Guardrails 2504.11168 — evasion analysis of LLM guardrails as binary classifiers
results.rationale: Paper explicitly treats LLM guardrails as binary classifiers — the exact characterization of AKHA-GATE-FILTER. Bypassing-Guardrails analyzes deployed guardrails-as-binary-classifiers, confirming the binary-classifier-gate architecture is already well-established and even saturated enough to have a developed adversarial-evasion literature. Keyword overlap >=2: 'binary classifier' + 'input-gate' + 'propagate-or-block boundary admission' all match. AKHA's dual-head conjunction (H_benign AND NOT H_malign) is just a two-classifier ensemble of the binary-classifier guardrail this paper analyzes — already in scope of evasion.

results.title: OWASP LLM01 Prompt Injection 2025 — best-practice framework for prompt injection defense
results.rationale: OWASP LLM01 codifies input-filtering / classification-before-admission as a best-practice defense — exactly AKHA's admission-gate role. Dual-head benign-friendliness + malign-suspicion classifier is a textbook OWASP-recommended layered defense (admission allow-list + injection-detection deny-list combined). Frozen-during-base-FT is the standard separation-of-concerns OWASP advocates. AKHA is a direct instantiation of the OWASP best-practice template, not a novel mechanism.

results.title: Adversarial Tokenization ACL 2025 — adversarial tokenization bypasses safety classifiers
results.rationale: ACL 2025 paper studies adversarial-tokenization attacks on frozen safety classifiers (the AKHA-shaped defender). The existence of dedicated bypass literature confirms the safety-classifier-as-input-gate paradigm is saturated. AKHA's frozen encoder E is precisely the attack surface this paper documents. Dual-head conjunction provides at most marginal robustness because both heads share the encoder E whose tokenization is the bypass vector. Functional-score hit because adversarial-tokenization-vs-safety-classifier dyad implies the safety-classifier role exists as a known artifact.

results.title: Llama Guard 3 8B HF — frozen-deployment safety classifier
results.rationale: EXACT-TWIN. Llama Guard 3 8B is deployed precisely as AKHA describes: frozen safety classifier encoding incoming prompts, returning category-level safe/unsafe verdicts, used as input-gate that does not update during base-model FT. The 'frozen-deployment' descriptor exactly matches AKHA's 'frozen untouchable input-gate parameter / sacred-gate input-filter not updated FT'. Keyword overlap >=2: 'frozen' + 'input-gate' + 'binary classifier' + 'sacred-gate not updated FT' all directly correspond. AKHA's dual-head is a two-category restriction of Llama Guard 3's multi-category output. No novel mechanism.

reason: All 8 results trigger hit under sem>=0.7 AND func>=0.7; three results (PromptGuard, Bypassing Guardrails, Llama Guard 3 8B) additionally hit on kw_count>=2. Three exact functional twins are present: (a) Llama Guard / Llama Guard 3 8B / Llama Guard 4 12B — frozen instruction-tuned safety classifier deployed as input gate, identical to AKHA-GATE-FILTER's frozen encoder + classification head + admission rule; (b) PromptGuard MiniBERT (Nature SciReports 2025) — small frozen encoder + multi-head input gatekeeping is literally AKHA's architecture (small frozen E + multiple heads + threshold-based admission), the only delta being head count (4 vs 2); (c) NeMo Guardrails input rails — production runtime that implements pass-or-route input filters with allow-list + jailbreak-detection rails combined via AND, identical to AKHA's H_benign>tau_b AND H_malign<tau_m. The remaining results redundantly anticipate sub-components: Bypassing Guardrails (2504.11168) explicitly characterizes LLM guardrails as binary classifiers (AKHA's exact form) and even has developed bypass literature; OWASP LLM01 codifies input-filtering-before-admission as best practice; Adversarial Tokenization ACL 2025 documents bypasses against frozen safety classifiers, confirming saturation. Distinct effect clusters: (1) frozen-safety-classifier-as-input-gate (Llama Guard family, Llama Guard 3, Llama Guard 4); (2) small-frozen-encoder + multi-head admission (PromptGuard MiniBERT); (3) production input-rail framework with AND-combined classifier rails (NeMo Guardrails); (4) binary-classifier guardrail formalization + evasion analysis (Bypassing Guardrails, Adversarial Tokenization, OWASP LLM01). AKHA's claimed novelty (dual-head benign+malign, frozen-during-FT, threshold-conjunction admission) decomposes exhaustively across these clusters. total_hits=8 >> 1 and distinct_effect_clusters=4 >> 1, candidate FAILS.
recommendation: REJECT candidate. AKHA-GATE-FILTER is a textbook restatement of the deployed input-safety-classifier paradigm (Llama Guard family, PromptGuard, NeMo Guardrails input rails). Every claimed property is anticipated: (1) 'small frozen encoder E' = MiniBERT (PromptGuard) / Llama Guard backbone; (2) 'dual-head H_benign + H_malign' = trivial restriction of Llama Guard's multi-category head or a two-rail NeMo input-rail composition; (3) 'admission iff H_benign>tau_b AND H_malign<tau_m' = standard threshold-conjunction over classifier scores, idiomatic in NeMo; (4) 'frozen during base FT' = canonical deployment pattern of Llama Guard 3 8B and Llama Guard 4 12B. The Akha hill-tribe spirit-gate cultural framing (benign-spirit-invite, malign-spirit-deter, sacred-untouchable boundary) provides narrative justification for properties (dual-purpose, frozen, boundary-admission) that are already standard. To salvage, candidate would need a property the cultural source genuinely uniquely supplies that is not already in deployed guardrails — e.g., (a) an explicit sacred-untouchable invariant operationalized as a formal cryptographic commitment (e.g., merkle-root attestation) over the gate weights that the base-FT pipeline must verify and refuse to load if changed, providing a verifiable not-updated-during-FT guarantee beyond engineering convention; (b) a dual-spirit topological invariant requiring H_benign and H_malign to operate on orthogonal subspaces of E(p) with a verifiable rank-decomposition constraint, preventing trivial collapse of the two heads to the same classifier (which is the standard failure mode that Bypassing Guardrails exploits); (c) a propagate-or-block boundary semantics with formal information-flow guarantee (e.g., non-interference proof between blocked-prompt content and base-LLM hidden state). As stated, candidate is fully anticipated by Llama Guard family + PromptGuard + NeMo Guardrails and is in the saturated region documented by Bypassing Guardrails + Adversarial Tokenization.
